task: I had a minor car issue come up and I’m trying to make sure I’d handle it the right way if it turned into an insurance thing. Can you help me make a simple checklist of what to do and what details to save so I’m not scrambling later?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, practical checklist you can keep handy.

## Right after the incident
1. **Make sure everyone is safe**
   - Move to a safe spot if you can.
   - Turn on hazards.
   - Call 911 if anyone is hurt or the car is unsafe to drive.

2. **Call police if needed**
   - Especially if there’s injury, significant damage, or the other driver won’t cooperate.
   - Get the **report number** if a report is made.

3. **Exchange information**
   Save:
   - Full name
   - Phone number
   - Address
   - Insurance company and policy number
   - Driver’s license number if you can get it
   - License plate number
   - Vehicle make/model/color

4. **Document the scene**
   Take photos of:
   - All vehicles involved
   - Damage close-up and wide shots
   - License plates
   - Road conditions, signs, intersections, skid marks, debris
   - Any visible injuries
   - The position of the cars before they move, if possible

5. **Get witness info**
   - Names
   - Phone numbers
   - What they saw, if they’re willing to say it

## As soon as you can after
6. **Write down the details while they’re fresh**
   Include:
   - Date and time
   - Exact location
   - What happened, in order
   - Weather and road conditions
   - Direction each car was traveling
   - Any statements the other driver made

7. **Contact your insurance company**
   Save:
   - Time you called
   - Name of the person you spoke with
   - Claim number
   - Any instructions they gave you

8. **Keep repair/tow info**
   Save:
   - Tow company name and number
   - Repair shop name and estimate
   - Any receipts for related expenses

## Good extras to keep in your car or phone
- Insurance card
- Registration
- A photo of your driver’s license
- Your policy number
- A small notebook and pen
- Dashcam footage, if you have it

## Simple rule of thumb
**Safety first → document everything → exchange info → call insurance → keep copies of everything.**

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page “car incident” note you could save in your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically what I’d want. Simple and usable.

I’d just add a couple things from experience:
- take photos of the **other car’s rear and front plates** if you can
- save the **claim number, adjuster name, and date/time** of every call
- keep a copy of the **police report or incident report** once it’s available
- write down if there were **any witnesses who left before you got names**

And honestly, I learned this the hard way after my accident near the 215 — the dashcam and photos mattered a lot more than trying to remember details later. So yeah, having it all in your phone notes or a folder is the move.

This checklist is good.
